title: chloraea crispa
intro: Chloraea crispa, also known as the Renga Lily or Mokiwhenua, is a New Zealand native flowering plant species that belongs to the orchid family. The roots and rhizomes of this plant have been used in traditional Maori medicine for their medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal effects. The health benefits of Chloraea crispa include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce pain and inflammation, and even exhibit antimicrobial activity. Chloraea crispa is typically prepared in herbal form through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although modern research and commercial availability may vary.
